title: Man wanted on warrants for armed robbery
content:
Calgary, AB,
27
April
2026
|
12:43
America/Denver
Man wanted on warrants for armed robbery
We are asking the public to help us locate a man wanted in connection with an armed robbery in the northeast community of Rundle.
On Tuesday, Jan. 27, 2026, the victim began communicating with a man he met on a dating app and arranged to meet him in-person. It is believed the victim met with the man at his residence in northeast Calgary. During the interaction, the man produced a handgun and demanded cash. The victim complied, and the man fled the residence.
Investigators have identified the offender but have been unable to locate him
,
and we are seeking information from the public regarding his whereabouts. Investigators believe he may have ties to Ontario.
Ramone Alex DACRES, 29, is wanted for robbery with a firearm.
Anyone with information about DACRES’ whereabouts is asked to contact police by calling 403-266-1234. Tips can also be submitted anonymously to Crime Stoppers through any of the following methods:
TALK: 1-800-222-8477

title: Police seek assistance in identifying sexual assault suspect
content:
Calgary, AB,
27
April
2026
|
11:27
America/Denver
Police seek assistance in identifying sexual assault suspect
We are asking the public for their help in identifying an unknown man who is believed to be responsible for two separate sexual assaults that took place last week in the city’s southeast.
On Monday, April 20, 2026, at approximately 5:30 p.m., a woman was jogging in Fish Creek Park near Mallard Point when she was sexually assaulted by an unknown man who was riding an e-scooter. It is believed the man had been observing the woman near the on-site bathrooms before the incident occurred.
The suspect continued to ride the e-scooter towards the community of Douglasdale.
It is believed the man sexually assaulted another woman in the park approximately 20 minuets prior to this incident.
The suspect is described as being in his late teens to early 20s, approximately 6’ tall, with a light skin tone and blond hair. At the time of the incidents, he was wearing black pants, a red hooded sweatshirt with the hood pulled up over his head and a white backpack with colourful spots. He was riding the e-scooter while playing music out loud on a speaker.
Anyone who believes they are a victim of sexual assault is encouraged to report it regardless of when it occurred. Anyone with information about this incident is asked to contact police by calling 403-266-1234. Tips can also be submitted anonymously to Crime Stoppers through any of the following methods:
TALK: 1-800-222-8477
TYPE:
www.calgarycrimestoppers.org

Serious-injury collision under investigation
Our Traffic Unit is investigating a single-vehicle collision that occurred this morning, sending one man to the hospital.
At approximately 6:20 a.m., on Sunday, April 26, 2026, a 2017 Chevrolet Cruze, driven by a man in his 20s, was travelling southbound on Macleod Trail S. at 94 Avenue S.E., when it left the roadway and struck a traffic signal pole located on the centre median.
The driver, who was the sole occupant, was transported to the hospital in life-threatening condition and has since been upgraded to stable with life-altering injuries.
Speed and alcohol are being investigated as possible factors in the collision.

content:

Three charged in commercial break and enter series
Two men and one woman have been charged in relation to a series of commercial break and enters that resulted in the theft of property valued at approximately $234,910.
Between Jan. 5 and 26, 2026, it is believed four businesses in the northeast community of Vista Heights were broken into and had property stolen. On Tuesday, Jan. 27, and Friday, Jan. 30, 2026, search warrants were executed on two residences located in the 600 and 1000 blocks of 17 Avenue S.W., and two vehicles.
The searches resulted in the seizure of:
Rare coins
Laptops
Human teeth that had valuable metals removed
Tools and equipment consistent with melting down precious metals
The recovered items have an approximate value of $34,915.
As a result, investigators arrested and charged three people.
Steven Edward HART, 42, of Calgary is charged with:
Six counts of commercial break and enter
Seven counts of trafficking stolen property
13 counts of breaching a judicial order
He will next appear in court on Thursday, April 30, 2026.
Alston Jason VAN WEZEL, 48, of Calgary, is charged with:
12 counts of commercial break and enter
One count of drug possession
He will next appear in court on Monday, April 27, 2026.
Erin Lindsay EMERSON, 49, of Calgary, is charged with:
Two counts of drug possession
One count of trafficking stolen property
One count of possession of stolen property
One count of possession of identity documents
Three counts of breaching a judicial order
She will next appear in court today, Friday, April 24, 2026.
Anyone with information about these incidents is asked to contact police by calling 403-266-1234. Tips can also be submitted anonymously to Crime Stoppers through any of the following methods:

TYPE:
www.calgarycrimestoppers.org

title: Charges laid in bank impersonation frauds
content:
Calgary, AB,
22
April
2026
|
09:28
America/Denver
Charges laid in bank impersonation frauds
We have laid charges in an organized
fraud scheme
that resulted in the loss of approximately $1.3 million from Calgarians.
The scam involved offenders using technology to impersonate a victim’s financial institution over the phone. While posing as representatives of the victim’s bank, the scammers informed victims that unauthorized transactions had occurred and the victim’s accounts had been compromised. In many cases, the offenders possessed detailed personal and financial information about the victims.
During their interactions, victims were manipulated into providing sensitive banking credentials, including online logins and debit card PINs. After obtaining this information, victims were instructed to place their bank cards in an envelope for courier collection. Couriers retrieved the envelopes and delivered the cards to the offenders, who then withdrew large amounts of cash, and made purchases including gift cards and high-end items.
In 2025, we received more than 80 reports consistent with this scam, representing an estimated combined financial loss of approximately $1.3 million.
Our Financial Crimes Investigation Unit identified 15 of the impersonation scam files that were believed to be connected to the same group, which had occurred between March and August 2025.
After a thorough investigation, which included over 80 production orders and the review of a significant amount of physical and digital evidence, on Tuesday, April 14, 2026, we executed a search warrant on a residence in the 4700 block of Dalton Drive N.W. Two men were arrested and the search resulted in the seizure of:
More than $59,000 in cash
Multiple debit, credit and identification cards
A small amount of cocaine
On Wednesday, April 15, 2026, investigators also executed a search warrant on a residence located in the 14600 block of 52 Street N.W., in Edmonton, Alta., where a third man was arrested.
Sirak HABTOM, 22, of Ottawa, Ogyeth MAYUM, 22, of Ottawa and Brian-Shodu KINYAMBA, 25, of Edmonton, are charged with one count each of:
Fraud over $5,000
Unauthorized use of a credit card
Money laundering
Theft over $5,000
At the time of his arrest, MAYUM was also wanted on 10 Canada-wide warrants for weapons offences in Ontario.
HABTOM will appear in court on Friday, April 24, 2026. KINYAMBA will appear in court on Thursday, May 7, 2026. MAYUM will appear in court on Thursday, May 14.
Investigators are continuing to review evidence in the case and additional charges may be laid.
“
“This investigation points to a far more extensive fraud operation and intelligence suggests this network extends well beyond Calgary. Fraud investigations are complex and resource-intensive, with most investigations involving large volumes of digital and financial data, multiple jurisdictions and offenders who deliberately use technology to exploit public trust. Despite these challenges, our investigators remain committed to disrupting organized fraud networks and continuing to warn the public about these evolving scams.”
Staff Sgt. Peter Duchnij of the CPS Financial Crimes Investigation Unit
„
Investigators would like to thank members of the Edmonton Police Service, Ontario Provincial Police, Ottawa Police Service and Peel Regional Police Service for their support throughout this investigation.
Anyone with information about these incidents is asked to contact police by calling 403-266-1234. Tips can also be submitted anonymously to Crime Stoppers through any of the following methods:
TALK: 1-800-222-8477
TYPE:
www.calgarycrimestoppers.org
APP: P3 Tips
Case #CA25179702/5600
How to protect yourself
Calgarians are reminded of the following tips to
protect themselves
from financial scams:
Be suspicious of unexpected and unusual phone calls, especially ones demanding some form of urgent action, financial or otherwise.
Do not share personal details, financial information or send money to the unknown caller.
Our officers or banks will never ask you to transfer money, cryptocurrency or gift cards as part of an investigation or suggest transactions as a way to guard your finances.
If you are suspicious of a call regarding your finances, compromised cards or an unsolicited courier service, contact your bank or financial institution using a verified number on their website or app, or visit a branch in-person.

title: Update – Police lay charges in Bridlewood arson investigation
content:
Calgary, AB,
21
April
2026
|
16:04
America/Denver
Update – Police lay charges in Bridlewood arson investigation
Our Arson Unit has charged a man in relation to an arson involving a home and detached garage in Bridlewood earlier this month.
At approximately 3 a.m. on Wednesday, April 1, 2026, we were called for reports of a fire involving a detached garage at a residence in the 100 block of Bridlewood Avenue S.W. Upon further investigation, our Arson Unit determined that two individuals entered the rear yard of the residence, deliberately set fire to the garage and attempted to set fire to the home before fleeing the area in a waiting vehicle.
As the investigation progressed, officers determined that the suspect vehicle involved was stolen. The District 6 operations team located the suspect with the stolen vehicle and arrested the suspect.
Investigators seized multiple items from the vehicle, including a shotgun, fentanyl and a hatchet. Clothing believed to be consistent with what was worn by one of the suspects at the time of the arson was also recovered.
As a result, Michael Keith CASELEY, 49, has been charged with:
Arson with disregard for human life
Possession of drugs
Possession of stolen property over $5,000
Possession of stolen property under $5,000
Driving while disqualified
Traffic offenses
CASELEY is facing additional charges pending examination of the firearm found in the vehicle at the time he was arrested. He is scheduled to next appear in court on Wednesday, April 22, 2026.
This investigation remains ongoing and anyone with information about this incident is asked to contact police by calling 403-266-1234. Tips can also be submitted anonymously to Crime Stoppers through any of the following methods:
Anyone with information about this incident is asked to contact police by calling 403-266-1234. Tips can also be submitted anonymously to Crime Stoppers through any of the following methods:

TYPE:
www.calgarycrimestoppers.org
APP: P3 Tips

title: Two additional men charged in March extortion shooting
content:
Calgary, AB,
20
April
2026
|
10:01
America/Denver
Two additional men charged in March extortion shooting
Earlier this month we announced charges in relation to a series of extortion shootings that took place in February and March. Last week, two additional men were charged.
On Sunday, Feb. 22, 2026, at around 2:45 a.m., police were called for reports of a shooting outside of a residence in the 8400 block of Saddleridge Drive N.E. It is believed that two suspects fired multiple rounds into the residence from a vehicle before fleeing the scene prior to police arrival.
On Wednesday, March 4, 2026, at around 5:10 a.m., the same residence was once again targeted, and shots were fired toward the residence by three suspects inside a vehicle.
After identifying a possible suspect vehicle during a traffic stop on Saturday, March 7, 2026, investigators analyzed evidence from the stop alongside information from earlier incidents. This work helped establish links between suspects, vehicles and a series of extortion-related shootings.
On Friday, April 3, 2026,
police laid charges against one individual
in relation to the investigation while continuing to pursue the additional suspects.
Last week, investigators arrested a second man in Edmonton and issued warrants for a third.
Karanbir SINGH, 21, has been charged with one count of discharging a firearm with intent. He was remanded into custody and will appear in court today, Monday, April 20, 2026.
Germanjeet SINGH, 22, is wanted on a warrant for discharging a firearm with intent. He is described as 5’8” tall, 198 pounds, with brown eyes and black hair. Photos of him is available on The City of Calgary Newsroom.
26-04-20 Germanjeet SINGH_2
26-04-20 Germanjeet SINGH
Download Media Kit
Preparing your download...
Download
An error occurred while preparing your download
“
“These additional charges underscore the strength of our partnerships with our policing partners not only here in the province but also nationally. Through collaborative efforts and shared intelligence, we are sending a clear message that there is no room for extortion or violent criminal activity in our city or anywhere in our province. We committed to holding those responsible accountable, and this investigation demonstrates that we will follow through on that commitment.”
Acting Deputy Chief Jeff Bell, Bureau of Investigative Support, Calgary Police Service
„
“
“We know that the criminal networks responsible for these violent extortions do not restrict themselves to one jurisdiction, and neither do we. The EPS continues to work with CPS, the RCMP and other law enforcement partners across the country to share information that helps identify and bring these suspects to justice and protect the safety of our communities.”
Deputy Chief Nicole Chapdelaine,  Investigations Bureau, Edmonton Police Service
„
“
"The Canada Border Services Agency (CBSA) collaborates closely with its law enforcement partners, including the Calgary Police Service and the Edmonton Police Service, to uphold public safety and ensure accountability for individuals who violate the law. The CBSA is committed to the swift removal of individuals who have misused Canada’s immigration system and pose risks to the safety and well-being of our communities."
Regional Director General Janalee Bell-Boychuk, Prairie Region, Canada Border Services Agency
„
We would like to thank our partners at the Edmonton Police Service, the Canada Border Services Agency and the Alberta Law Enforcement Response Team for their help with this investigation.
Since January 2025, there have been 41 extortion attempts in Calgary. Seventeen of the incidents involved shootings at homes, businesses, or vehicles. These crimes are happening across Canada, and while they have involved threats and shootings, we have no reported injuries in Calgary to date.
If you have information in relation to this series of extortions, please contact police by calling 403-266-1234. Tips can also be submitted anonymously to Crime Stoppers though any of the following methods:
TALK: 1-800-222-8477
TYPE: www.calgarycrimestoppers.org
APP: P3 Tips
Case # CA26094229/3750

title: Seeking public assistance in shooting investigation
content:
Calgary, AB,
16
April
2026
|
15:52
America/Denver
Seeking public assistance in shooting investigation
We are looking for information from the public after two men attended a northeast Calgary hospital with gunshot wounds last month.
Just after 3 a.m., on Saturday, March 28, 2026, two men attended the Peter Lougheed Centre, located at 3500 26 Ave. N.E., suffering from gunshot wounds. Patrol officers circulated multiple areas attempting to locate the scene of the shooting, however, were unsuccessful. No shootings were reported to police.
The victims have since been released from hospital.
Investigators have been able to determine that the victims had attended an event at the Empire Banquet Hall, located at 4826 11 St. N.E., earlier that night. After a review of CCTV, it is believed the victims left the banquet hall just after midnight and it remains unknown where or when the shooting took place.
At this stage, we believe the shooting was likely targeted, however we are looking for anyone with information about this incident, including where the shooting occurred, to come forward.
“The presence of firearms in our community is a significant concern. Targeted or not, shootings put our entire community at risk,” says Det. Kim Zinn of the CPS District 4 General Investigations Unit. “We are asking anyone with information to contact police. Sometimes even the smallest piece of information can be the puzzle piece we need.”
Our efforts to address gun violence rely on strong community support. If you have information that could help an investigation, such as witnessing suspicious behaviour or vehicles, please contact police.
Anyone with information is asked to contact police by calling 403-266-1234. Tips can also be submitted anonymously to Crime Stoppers through any of the following methods:
TALK: 1-800-222-8477
TYPE:
www.calgarycrimestoppers.org
APP: P3 Tips

We are asking for the public’s help to identify a man believed to be responsible for an arson involving a home and detached garage in Bridlewood earlier this month.

Police investigate fatal collision
Our Traffic Section is investigating the circumstances of a fatal collision that occurred Sunday night.
On Sunday, April 12, 2026, at approximately 8:20 p.m., a
man
who remains unidentified at this time
crossed 16 Avenue, near 8 Street S.W., from Tomkins Park. Before reaching the curb, the man fell or laid down on the roadway in front of a parked Nissan Rogue. Several minutes later, the driver of the Rogue returned to their vehicle, started the engine and pulled out from the parking spot, driving over the pedestrian.
The pedestrian was taken to hospital where he has since passed away. An autopsy is being conducted by The Office of the Chief Medical Examiner to determine whether the man passed away from injuries sustained during the collision.
The driver, a man in his 50s, remained at the scene and was uninjured.
The investigation is ongoing.
Anyone with information about this incident is asked to contact police by calling 403-266-1234. Tips can also be submitted anonymously to Crime Stoppers through any of the following methods:

title: Weapon incident at Mount Royal University resolved
content:
Calgary, AB,
13
April
2026
|
17:39
America/Denver
Weapon incident at Mount Royal University resolved
We have safely resolved an incident that occurred this afternoon at Mount Royal University.
At approximately 2:45 p.m., today, Monday, April 13, 2026, we responded to a report of an individual carrying a large knife at Mount Royal University (MRU), located at 4825 Mount Royal Gate S.W. Police contained the area and after confirming the witness reports, a shelter in place order was issued for the university and surrounding communities.
While in the process of clearing the scene, officers received information that the individual was a staff member working as a chef for the university. After verifying this information, the shelter in place was lifted.
“This incident highlights how seriously we treat reports of potential threats. Our officers responded quickly to ensure the safety of the campus and surrounding community,” says Insp. Darren Smith of the CPS Real Time Operations Centre. “Although we now know the public was not in danger, our officers are well trained for these situations.
This investigation ongoing and charges may be laid. One man was taken into custody from the scene.
We appreciate the cooperation of Mount Royal University staff and the public while the situation was resolved.
Statement from Mount Royal University:
“We appreciate the prompt response from the CPS as we worked closely with them throughout their response. We understand the stress this incident has caused our students and employees and are grateful for this positive outcome.”
Case # CA26158350

title: Charges laid in break and enter series targeting Calgary businesses
content:
Calgary, AB,
10
April
2026
|
08:25
America/Denver
Charges laid in break and enter series targeting Calgary businesses
We have charged two men with 67 criminal offences and recovered firearms, drugs and stolen property in relation to a break and enter series that occurred earlier this year.
Between Saturday, Jan. 3, 2026, and Wednesday, Jan. 21, 2026, seven commercial break and enters occurred throughout Calgary at gaming, electronics and liquidation stores. During the incidents offenders smashed the front of the stores, causing significant damage, and stole various items, including gaming cards, electronics and knives. The total dollar loss between the break and enters was estimated to be $30,000, which includes the stolen items and property damages.
Investigators from the District 4 Break and Enter Team were able to link the break and enters to the same suspects, and on Wednesday, Feb. 11, 2026, officers executed a search warrant on a residence located in the 1400 block of 42 Street N.E.
As a result of the search, police seized:
A large quantity of gaming cards
Three firearms, including a prohibited handgun, a sawed-off shotgun and a rifle
An extended magazine
A taser
$35,000 worth of drugs, including fentanyl, methamphetamine, cocaine and psilocybin
Three stolen vehicles
T
hree stolen bicycles
A stolen licence plate
31 stolen government issued identification documents
Multiple vehicle keys and devices used to duplicate key fobs
Devices used to create fraudulent documents
Two men have since been arrested and charged.
Chad SNIDER, 38, of Calgary, is charged with 49 offences, including hazardous storage of a firearm, possession of a restricted weapon, possession of a restricted weapon with readily available ammunition, unauthorized possession of a firearm, possession of a controlled substance for the purpose of trafficking, possession of stolen property, break and enter, disguise with intent, possession of identity documents and failing to comply with conditions of a release order. SNIDER will next appear in court on Friday, April 17, 2026.
Coady Philip MACINTYRE, 37, of Calgary, is charged with 18 offences including break and enter, disguise with intent and failing to comply with conditions of an undertaking. MACINTYRE will next appear in court on Monday, April 13, 2026.
“This was a concerning series of break and enters that caused substantial loss and disruption to seven Calgary businesses,” says S/Sgt. Keith Shufelt of the CPS District 4 General Investigations Unit. “Through diligent investigative work, our officers were able to hold those responsible accountable, while recovering stolen property and removing dangerous weapons from the street.”

title: Charge laid after February altercation
content:
Calgary, AB,
09
April
2026
|
17:23
America/Denver
Charge laid after February altercation
One man has been charged after an altercation outside of a place of worship in February.
On Saturday, Feb. 7, 2026, at around 6 p.m., a religious event was being hosted at a place of worship, located in the 400 block of Meridian Road S.E. Ahead of the scheduled event, approximately 12 people arrived to protest the gathering in light of recent geopolitical events. It is believed that people associated with the centre then exited the building and verbally threatened the protestors before another man driving an SUV steered toward the group of protestors, striking one person.
The victim suffered minor injuries as a result of being struck by the vehicle.
Due to the intersection of political and religious elements, our Hate Crime Prevention Team launched an investigation into the incident.
After a thorough investigation including witness accounts and CCTV footage, one person has been charged.
Payman ESMAEILI, 49, of Calgary, has been charged with one count of assault with a weapon.
He will next appear in court on Friday, May 22, 2026.
Whether hate motivation was a factor in the altercation remains under investigation.
“We recognize that this incident is closely connected to deeply felt political and human rights tensions internationally, and that members of our community hold strong and differing views,” says Calgary Police Service acting Supt. Cory Ma. “Our role is not to take sides in political disputes, however when the incident involves threats, violence or intimidation, it is our responsibility to conduct a fair and objective investigation.”
Anyone with information about this incident is asked to contact police by calling 403-266-1234. Tips can also be submitted anonymously to Crime Stoppers through any of the following methods:
TALK: 1-800-222-8477

www.calgarycrimestoppers.org
APP: P3 Tips

title: Calgary Police Service member charged in breach of privacy investigati..
content:
Calgary, AB,
09
April
2026
|
12:56
America/Denver
Calgary Police Service member charged in breach of privacy investigation
Following an investigation by the Calgary Police Service Sensitive Investigations Unit, a civilian member of the Service has been charged for intentionally accessing police databases for non-police matters.
In December 2025, police were made aware of allegations that protected information had been accessed. It is believed a member of the Calgary Police Service was accessing police databases against policy and authorized use to seek information on individuals she has had, or sought to have, personal relationships with. An investigation began and investigators identified a woman who was believed to be leveraging her access to police data and facilities between Feb. 1 and Aug. 1, 2025, to retrieve protected information.
On Wednesday, March 4, 2026, a search warrant was executed, and the woman’s mobile device was seized. The investigation identified four victims who had their data targeted and intentionally searched. The impacted individuals have been notified.
“We recognize that a breach of this nature shakes the foundation of trust that we have built with the public and our members. Allegations such as these are taken very seriously, and we are committed to addressing this matter in a thorough and appropriate manner,” says Insp. Dehl Vella of the Calgary Police Service Professional Standards Section.
On Tuesday, April 7, 2026, Kayla JESSEN was arrested and charged with nine counts under the Protection of Privacy Act section 60(1)(a) for collecting, using or disclosing information in contravention of Part 1 of the Act and section 60(1)(b) gaining or attempting to gain access to personal information in contravention of the Act.
JESSEN is a three-year employee of the Calgary Police Service and is currently on an unrelated leave from the Service pending review.
She was released from custody and will next appear in court on Tuesday, June 2, 2026.
Anyone with information regarding this incident is asked to contact police by calling 403-266-1234.
Case # SIU25-084/4367

Missing person - Elijah
We are asking for the public’s help to locate a man who has been reported missing from the downtown area.

At this time, we have exhausted all investigative avenues to locate him and are now turning to the public for help.
There is currently no indication that Elijah’s absence is the result of foul play. He is known to frequent the downtown area.
Elijah is described as 5’3” tall, about 130 pounds with brown hair and brown eyes.

Need to identify suspect in northeast hit-and-run investigation
We are asking for the public’s help to identify a driver who was involved in a pedestrian hit-and-run in the northeast community of Taradale.
At approximately 7:10 a.m., on Tuesday, March 10, 2026, members were called to the intersection of Tarington Way and Taradale Drive N.E. for reports of a hit-and-run. Upon arrival, officers located a boy in medical distress.
It is believed the boy was crossing the road at a marked crosswalk when a white, four-door sedan entered the intersection and struck him. The car drove off without stopping. Bystanders assisted the boy, and he was transported to hospital in stable condition.
Investigators are asking anyone who may have witnessed the incident, or who has dashcam footage from the area at the time of the incident, to contact police by calling 403-266-1234. Tips can also be submitted anonymously to Crime Stoppers through any of the following methods:

title: Information sought after $43,000 spending spree using stolen wallet
content:
Calgary, AB,
12

Information sought after $43,000 spending spree using stolen wallet
We are asking for the public’s help to identify a suspect connected to a wallet theft that led to more than $43,000 in unauthorized transactions.
On Thursday, Nov. 27, 2025, between 11:15 a.m. and 12:30 p.m., it is believed that an unknown suspect stole a man’s wallet while he was having lunch near the 200 block of Barclay Parade S.W.
Investigators believe the victim had briefly placed his jacket on the back of his chair when the suspect approached from behind and removed the wallet from the jacket pocket.
The victim did not realize his wallet had been taken until after the suspect left the area.
Shortly after leaving the restaurant, the suspect is believed to have made multiple fraudulent online purchases using the stolen cards.
Soon afterward, the victim received several phone calls from individuals posing as employees of the Royal Bank of Canada’s Fraud Department who were attempting to obtain additional personal and financial information.
The suspect is described as a man in his late 20s to early 30s, about 5’11” to 6’ tall, with a medium build and a short beard. At the time of the incident, he was wearing a black hooded jacket, grey Nike sweatpants, white shoes and a black baseball cap.
Anyone who knows the identity of the suspect, or who believes they have been a victim of fraud, is asked to contact police by calling 403-266-1234. Tips can also be submitted anonymously to Crime Stoppers through any of the following methods:
TALK: 1-800-222-8477
TYPE:

title: 23 charges laid in stolen-merchandise trafficking investigation
content:

23 charges laid in stolen-merchandise trafficking investigation
A man has been charged following an investigation into the trafficking and sale of stolen high-end merchandise from a Calgary retail store.
On Tuesday, March 10, 2026, Lululemon security notified police that an individual was selling stolen merchandise on Facebook Marketplace for roughly half the original price.
As the investigation progressed, officers identified the individual and executed a search warrant at his residence on Saturday, March 28, 2026. During the search, officers seized new Lululemon clothing, a significant number of forged identity documents and 17 pieces of stolen mail.
As a result, Tyler Anthony CHASSE, 23, of Calgary, is charged with 23 offences, including:
·
One count of possession of stolen property
·
One count of trafficking stolen property
·
One count of possession of proceeds of crime
·
Two counts of possession of stolen mail
·
18 counts of possession of forged documents
CHASSE is scheduled to next appear in court on Friday, April 10, 2026.
Retail crime may appear victimless, but it has real consequences for business owners and affects the overall sense of safety in our community. Fraud can also carry significant emotional and financial impacts, and no one should feel embarrassed or alone in coming forward.
Anyone who believes they may be a victim of fraud is encouraged to report it to the Calgary Police Service or Crime Stoppers.
Anyone with information about this incident is asked to contact police by calling 403-266-1234. Tips can also be submitted anonymously to Crime Stoppers through any of the following methods:
TALK: 1-800-222-8477

title: Charges laid in copper theft from medical supply business
content:
Calgary, AB,
08
April
2026
|

Charges laid in copper theft from medical supply business
We have charged one man following a copper pipe theft that caused significant damage to a medical supply facility last summer.
On Thursday, July 24, 2025, a medical supply business located in the 400 block of 19 Street S.E., contacted police to report a break and enter at its production facility, where temperature-sensitive, lifesaving dialysis materials were stored. It is believed that a man entered the site multiple times over the course of a week, stealing custom vehicle rims and copper piping from refrigeration units used to cool the company’s warehouse. The theft caused the refrigeration units to fail, resulting in approximately $400,000 in damages.
Following a media report about the incident, a member of the public came forward with information. After an extensive investigation, officers were able to identify and locate an offender.
Peter Roy THEDORFF, 52, of Calgary, has been charged with three counts of break and enter and one count of mischief. THEDORFF will next appear in court on Thursday, April 30, 2026.
“
The damage extended well beyond the theft itself, disrupting the storage of lifesaving medical products and creating real impacts in our community. We are grateful to the member of public who came forward with information to help investigators hold this individual accountable.
Const. Brad Vegso of the CPS District 4 Break and Enter Team
„
Case # CA25319048/5929

Encampment removed following joint safety operation in Foothills County
A collaborative safety operation involving the Calgary Police Service (CPS), Okotoks Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) and Foothills County Peace Officers has resulted in the removal of an encampment located just outside Calgary city limits, following ongoing concerns about violence, property crime and public safety in the surrounding area.
The encampment, situated about one kilometre outside of Calgary on private rural land near 210 Avenue and 64 Street S.W., had generated numerous calls for service between Thursday, Jan. 1 and Sunday, March 1, 2026, including reports of unsafe behaviour, suspicious persons and thefts. RCMP and Foothills County Peace Officers also responded to reports of individuals from the encampment who had chased or struck passing vehicles and, in one case, threatened a road maintenance worker with a knife.
On Monday, March 23, 2026, officers from the CPS District 8 Community Engagement Response Team (CERT), Okotoks RCMP and Foothills County Peace Officers attended the site to address safety concerns and support cleanup efforts. One individual was located within a structure and arrested on outstanding warrants and trespassing offences. Officers offered support services, including transportation to Calgary to access those resources.
On Tuesday, March 24, 2026, officers returned to ensure the safety of cleanup crews and again located the same individual within the encampment. The individual was arrested for breaching release conditions. Despite multiple attempts by partner clinicians to engage, the individual declined available supports.
During the cleanup, crews removed four trailer loads of debris and items, including makeshift structures and assorted property such as two bicycles and several small household items. Four knives were also seized, and no other weapons were located.
“While we lead with compassion and prioritize connecting people to community resources, public safety remains our priority,” says CPS District 8 Staff Sgt. Graeme Smiley. “Violence and unsafe behaviour will not be tolerated, and we are grateful to our partner agencies for their commitment to working together to keep our communities safe.”
Together with our partners, we will continue concentrated efforts to enhance public safety in locations experiencing elevated levels of social disorder, criminal activity and unsafe behaviour. Our goal is to strengthen our city together for a Safer Calgary.
Case #:
CA26125481/CA26123900/4788

title: Police investigate serious-injury collision on Crowchild Trail
content:
Calgary, AB,
06
April
2026
|
16:11
America/Denver
Police investigate serious-injury collision on Crowchild Trail
Our Traffic Section continues to investigate after a serious-injury collision left a woman in her 40s in life-threatening condition earlier this morning.
At approximately 6:40 a.m., today, Monday, April 6, 2026, a two-vehicle collision occurred in the southbound lanes of Crowchild Trail near University Drive N.W.
At that time, a white 2024 Toyota Corolla, driven by a man in his 30s, with a woman in her 40s as a rear passenger, was travelling southbound on Crowchild Trail N.W. It is believed the Corolla collided with the rear of a 2010 Toyota Yaris, driven by a woman also in her 40s.
Investigators believe the Corolla went through a traffic barrier and left the roadway and dropped down onto the northbound lanes of Crowchild Trail N.W.
As a result of the collision, the passenger in the Corolla sustained life-altering injuries and was transported to hospital in life-threatening condition. The driver of the Corolla sustained serious injuries and was also transported to hospital. The driver of the Yaris was uninjured.
At this time, there is no evidence of alcohol or drugs being a factor in this collision.
Police are investigating if speed was a factor in relation to the adverse weather conditions present at the time of the collision.
Anyone with information about this incident is asked to contact police by calling 403-266-1234. Tips can also be submitted anonymously to Crime Stoppers through any of the following methods:
TALK: 1-800-222-8477

Missing person - Clayton
We are asking for the public’s help with any information about the disappearance of Clayton Condos, who was 20 years old at the time he went missing over 20 years ago. Clayton was last seen on Monday, March 20, 2006, in the northwest community of Brentwood, near Brentwood Mall.
Since that time, there has been no contact or confirmed activity, and investigators believe the disappearance to be suspicious in nature.
Although over two decades have passed, every case matters and no one is forgotten. Investigators remain deeply committed to seeking answers and pursuing justice, no matter how much time has gone by. Historical investigations are actively reviewed as new tools, technology, and investigative techniques continue to evolve, often providing opportunities to re
‑
examine evidence and uncover new leads.
No matter how dated someone’s information may be, we are asking anyone with information about this unsolved investigation to come forward. Even small details can shed new light.

title: Police warn Calgarians of paving scams
content:
Calgary, AB,

April
2026

Police warn Calgarians of paving scams
We are warning members of the public of an ongoing scam involving paving contractors in Calgary and the surrounding area.
These contractors will approach home and business owners and claim to have just finished another paving project in the area.
They will offer to pave the customer's property with free material and only charge for labour. Home or business owners are presented with a contract showing blank spaces for the cost per square foot and area covered by the project. When the customer signs the incomplete contract, the contractor paves a large area with a very thin layer of poor quality asphalt and then completes the square footage and price on the already signed contract.
In some cases, contractors attempt to intimidate customers into paying immediately for the work , and provide false addresses and contact information.
Calgary Police advise people to research companies through the Better Business Bureau, Consumer Investigations Unit, and customer reviews before accepting any paving offers, and to contact police if contractors make threats against any person’s safety.
Anyone with information about these scams is asked to contact police by calling 403-266-1234. Tips can also be submitted anonymously to Crime Stoppers through any of the following methods:
TALK: 1-800-222-8477
TYPE:
www.calgarycrimestoppers.org
APP: P3 Tips
